preamble: The diphthong /oi/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to /ei/, primarily through a process of unrounding and fronting of its initial vowel component. This shift involves the rounded back-mid vowel /o/ becoming the unrounded front-mid vowel /e/, while the second element of the diphthong, /i/, remains consistent.
phoneticEffects: Unrounding, Fronting of the initial vowel element of the diphthong

examples.note: In unstressed final syllables, particularly in the nominative and dative plural endings of o-stem nouns.

sources: Weiss, M. (2009). Outline of the Historical and Comparative Grammar of Latin. Beech Stave Press.
Allen, W.S. (1965). Vox Latina: A Guide to the Pronunciation of Classical Latin. Cambridge University Press.
